note:
No HOT setups today? You gotta be kidding me. Zero. Nada. Zilch. When was the last time we didn’t have even one name lighting it up on the squeeze radar? This is what total indecision looks like. The market is just standing there, arms crossed, waiting for someone to make the first move. And the bears? They’ve got nothing. If they did, we’d see it in the charts, but instead, the scoreboard is silent. Here’s your cheat code: when nobody’s squeezing, it doesn’t mean nothing is happening. It means tension is building.

So what’s the market trying to tell us? Risk is firmly neutral, full stop. Forget about trending sectors or macro themes for a minute—when *everything* cools down, it’s a waiting game for the next catalyst. Maybe it’s Powell, maybe it’s earnings, maybe a headline out of China. But until we get that “go” signal, the game plan is clear: tighten up your stops, manage your winners, and don’t overthink it. The fact that no sectors, no industries, no single names are breaking loose right now doesn’t mean we’re out of plays. It just means we need to focus on what’s already working.

The leaders in our portfolio are doing their job. Look at $MP up 122%, $UUUU up 133%, $COIN up 140%. That’s leadership. These aren’t just trades; these are textbook examples of how to dominate in a neutral market. You buy the breakout, sell half into strength, and trail the rest. Keep it simple. And it’s not too late for late entries on a few of these. $NIO’s still climbing out of the basement with 2 entries locked in, $CF in ag’s showing solid gains, and $SMR in nuclear is quietly outperforming. You don’t have to swing for the fences every time—sometimes a single or double is all you need.

But what really gets me is $BTU. Coal, of all things. Entry was $2.45, max gain 100%, and we still have half the position riding. You know what that says? The energy trade isn’t dead; it's just lurking. Same with $OXY. Two separate positions, both green, both steady. The market’s whispering, not shouting, and right now it’s telling us to keep an eye on energy names. Not chasing, not piling in—just watching.

So what’s the watchlist for today? Keep an eye on China tech: $LI, $NIO, $GDS. These names have momentum, but no one’s giving them credit yet. Same with crypto: $ETHA at 63% gains and $BLSH at 186% are proof the blockchain trade isn’t as dead as Twitter might think. And speaking of the forgotten trades, don’t sleep on uranium. $UUUU, $UEC, $SMR—they’re not done yet. Period.

Today’s not about new setups, it’s about navigating the quiet. Nothing flashy, just managing what’s working and staying locked in for when the squeeze machine kicks back into gear. The tension is building, and when it snaps, you better believe we’ll be ready. Are you?
